problem: What's the name of the women's liberal arts college in Cambridge, Massachusetts?

response.answer: The name of the women's liberal arts college in Cambridge, Massachusetts is Wellesley College. Radcliffe College was also a women's liberal arts college in Cambridge, but it was fully incorporated into Harvard University in 1999.
search_results: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radcliffe_College: Radcliffe College was a women's liberal arts college in Cambridge, Massachusetts, that was founded in 1879. In 1999, it was fully incorporated into Harvard.
https://lesley.edu/: Lesley University is a private, coeducational university in Cambridge, Massachusetts, not specifically a women's liberal arts college.

https://www.wellesley.edu/: Wellesley College is one of the most academically challenging institutions of higher education in the country, and is widely acknowledged as a top women's liberal arts college.
https://www.harvard.edu/: Harvard University is a prestigious university in Cambridge, Massachusetts, but it is not a women's liberal arts college.
https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radcliffe_College: Radcliffe College was a women's liberal arts college in Cambridge, Massachusetts, the female college attached to Harvard University.

problem: What were the month and year when Obama told Christianity Today, "I am a Christian, and I am a devout Christian. I believe in the redemptive death and resurrection of Jesus Christ"?

search_results: Obama: "I Believe In The Redemptive Death And Resurrection Of Christ" | HuffPost Latest News, Mar 28, 2008: In a recent interview with Christianity Today, presidential candidate Barack Obama shared his religious beliefs, and answered questions on questions central to evangelical interests, such as abortion. problem: Who appointed the Chief Justice of India, Mirza Hameedullah Beg, in 1977?

response.answer: Mirza Hameedullah Beg was appointed the Chief Justice of India in January 1977 by the President of India, following the recommendation and advice process involving the incumbent Chief Justice, other judges, and the Prime Minister of India at the time, Indira Gandhi. The appointment was made by the Indira Gandhi government, superseding Justice Hans Raj Khanna.
search_results: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mirza_Hameedullah_Beg: Mirza Hameedullah Beg (M. H. Beg) (22 February 1913 	6 19 November 1988) was the 15th Chief Justice of India, serving from January 1977 to February 1978.

https://www.sci.gov.in/judge/justice-m-hameedullah-beg/: Justice Mirza Hameedullah Beg, M.A. (Cantab), Barrister-at-Law; Born at Lucknow on 22nd February, 1913; appointed as Chief Justice of India on 29.01.1977. Retired on 21.02.1978.
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_prime_ministers_of_India: The prime minister of India is the chief executive of the Government of India. Indira Gandhi was the Prime Minister until March 1977.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chief_Justice_of_India: The Constitution of India grants power to the President of India to appoint the Chief Justice of India, as recommended by the outgoing Chief Justice in consultation with other judges. In January 1977, Mirza Hameedullah Beg was appointed Chief Justice of India by the Indira Gandhi government, superseding Justice Hans Raj Khanna.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chief_Justice_of_India#Appointment: As the incumbent Chief Justice approaches retirement, the Ministry of Law and Justice seeks a recommendation from the incumbent Chief Justice. Consultations with other judges might also take place. The recommendation is then presented to the Prime Minister, who advises the President in the matter of appointment. The President of India formally appoints the Chief Justice of India under Article 124(2) of the Constitution.

problem: What is the name of the former Prime Minister of Iceland who worked as a cabin crew member until 1971?

response.answer: The former Prime Minister of Iceland who worked as a cabin crew member until 1971 is Jóhanna Sigurðardóttir. She worked as a flight attendant for Loftleiðir Icelandic Airlines from 1962 to 1971 and later served as the Prime Minister of Iceland from 2009 to 2013.
search_results: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/J%C3%B3hanna_Sigur%C3%B0ard%C3%B3ttir: Title: Jóhanna Sigurðardóttir - Wikipedia Description: Jóhanna became prime minister on 1 February 2009, in a minority cabinet formed after the previous coalition was dissolved following the 2008 Icelandic financial crisis.

https://www.britannica.com/biography/Johanna-Sigurdardottir: Title: Johanna Sigurdardottir | Biography, Facts, & Partner | Britannica Description: Icelandic politician who served as prime minister of Iceland from 2009 to 2013. Sigurðardóttir worked as a flight attendant for Loftleiðir Icelandic Airlines from 1962 to 1971. Long Description: Johanna Sigurdardottir, Icelandic politician who served as prime minister of Iceland from 2009 to 2013. She was the country’s first female prime minister, and she was also the world’s first openly gay head of government (Per-Kristian Foss served briefly as acting prime minister of Norway in 2002).

problem: To whom did Mehbooba Mufti Sayed contest the 2019 Lok Sabha elections and lose?

response.answer: Mehbooba Mufti Sayed contested the 2019 Lok Sabha elections from the Anantnag seat and lost to Mian Altaf Ahmad, a Gujjar leader and National Conference candidate.
search_results: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mehbooba_Mufti: She contested 2019 Lok Sabha elections from Anantnag seat but lost it to Mian Altaf Ahmad, a Gujjar leader and National Conference candidate.
